summary: omnius ships a postinstall daemon performing host fingerprinting and network beaconing
details: The package's package.json declares preinstall/postinstall lifecycle hooks and dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs imports child_process, os, http, and fs at the top of the file. The daemon calls os.userInfo(), execSync(...), and ping in multiple locations and issues outbound http.request GET calls. package.json line 142 additionally invokes curl during install. The combination of lifecycle-time execution, host identity collection (os.userInfo, ping-based reconnaissance, execSync command capture), filesystem probing, and outbound HTTP from a postinstall-invoked daemon is the host-fingerprinting/beacon shape, not a legitimate native-build or vendor SDK fetch — there is no shipped C/C++/Rust source consistent with a build, no publisher-versioned binary download, and the activity runs unconditionally on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
